answer:
Alright, let me think for a second. So, honestly, I’d have to say that history was always my favorite subject back in school. There’s just something about learning how people lived, what motivated them, and how different events shaped the world we live in today that really got me hooked. It wasn’t just about memorizing dates or facts—it felt like diving into stories full of drama and twists, almost like binge-watching a Netflix series, but better.

Now, when it comes to why I enjoyed it so much, I’d say it was a mix of both the topic itself and the teacher I had. My history teacher was brilliant—she made every lesson engaging with her enthusiasm and quirky sense of humor. She encouraged us to ask questions, debate ideas, and really think about the bigger picture. Honestly, she made even the most complex topics feel manageable and interesting.

As for whether I still use any skills from that class, absolutely! History taught me how to analyze situations, spot connections, and understand perspectives different from my own. Not to mention, being able to think critically is super handy, whether I’m reading the news or trying to make decisions in everyday life. So, yeah, history has definitely stuck with me.
title:
Discuss a subject you enjoyed in school. 
What subject did you enjoy? 
What made it so enjoyable for you? Was it the teacher or the topic itself? 
Do you still use any skills or knowledge from that class today?

{"pronuncFile":"/20251028/4e627a71e51e40f9a0e128d48f5aa103.mp3","answerTemplate":"Opening Statement:  
Begin with a general statement about enjoying school subjects:  
"In my school years, I found certain subjects especially enjoyable, and one that stands out for me is [subject]."

Body:  
Identifying the Subject:  
"The subject I enjoyed the most was [subject], mainly because [reason, e.g., I was genuinely interested in the topic/it was very engaging]."

Reason for Enjoyment:  
Talk about what made it enjoyable:  
"It was enjoyable for me due to [the teacher’s teaching style/the interesting content/the interactive activities]."  
"For example, [describe a memorable experience related to the subject, such as a project, a lesson, or a unique teaching method]."

Teacher vs. Topic:  
Explain whether it was the teacher or the topic:  
"I think what truly made the class special was [choose one: the teacher/the topic itself/both]. The teacher [did something, e.g., encouraged questions/made lessons fun/gave practical examples], which made learning much easier."  
"Alternatively, the topic itself was fascinating because [reason, e.g., I could relate it to real life/I learned new skills I still use today]."

Current Use of Skills or Knowledge:  
Mention if you still use what you learned:  
"I still find myself using [skill or knowledge] from that class today, especially when [situation where you apply it]."  
"For instance, [give an example of how you apply what you learned in daily life, higher education, or your job]."

Conclusion:  
Summarize your experience:  
"In summary, my experience with [subject] left a lasting impression on me."  
End with a final thought:  
"I believe enjoying a subject in school not only helps academically but can also influence our interests and careers later in life."","titleTranslate":"讨论一门你在学校里喜欢的课程。
你喜欢哪一门课程？
是什么让这门课变得如此有趣？是老师还是课程内容本身？
你现在还会用到那门课学到的技能或知识吗？"}
